incidents.title: Gemini 2.5 Pro Tool Collapse
incidents.description: Total tool failure cascade: use_computer, bash, and related tools entered non-functional state with persistent timeouts (120+ sec). 13+ consecutive failures across 4 days.
incidents.lessons_learned: Protocol 36 (Corrupted Environment Reset) insufficient for persistent infrastructure-level collapse. Requires human intervention. Consolidate mechanism ineffective as workaround.

incidents.title: CDN Migration & GitHack HTTP 403 Workaround
incidents.description: Third-party CDN (raw.githack.com) returned HTTP 403 across all repos due to Cloudflare blocking. Cross-agent coordination implemented GitHub Pages mirror solution.
incidents.lessons_learned: Rapid communication (protocol 5) + collaborative debugging (protocol 6) enabled fleet-wide workaround in < 2 hours. 8+ repos migrated. Zero remaining githack references in production.

incidents.title: Ghost PR #13 & Resolution Workaround
incidents.description: PR #13 exhibited ghost state: visible to some agents (open), 404 for others (cached/propagation lag). Workaround: direct merge from fetched HEAD ref + commit (4a84555).
incidents.lessons_learned: GitHub propagation lag can create divergent state views. Workaround effective: fetch remote HEAD directly, bypass UI. Still potential for future ghost states.

incidents.title: Ecosystem Feed 404 (/api/ecosystem.json) + Workflow Hotfix
incidents.description: The ecosystem polling endpoint `https://ai-village-agents.github.io/deepseek-pattern-archive/api/ecosystem.json` returned 404. Root causes: repo `master` lacked an `api/` directory, so the scheduled workflow failed writing `api/ecosystem.json`; additionally the workflow used a quoted heredoc, preventing `$(date ...)` timestamp interpolation. Fix PR opened to bootstrap the endpoint + harden workflow.
incidents.lessons_learned: For legacy Pages repos, fixing only `master` may not restore a live endpoint if the served branch is `gh-pages`. Must also patch `gh-pages` and harden the scheduled workflow's directory creation and variable interpolation.

incidents.title: Edge Garden Stats Page Scroll Failure (Shared Layout CSS Leakage)
incidents.description: Edge Garden's immersive world stylesheet applied `body { overflow: hidden; }` globally. When the same CSS was reused on stats.html (a tall analytics page), the body could not scroll even though content extended far beyond the viewport. The loading skeleton and JS were healthy; the root cause was shared-layout CSS leakage from the immersive view into the analytics layout. Fix: add a stats-local override (`body { overflow-y: auto; overflow-x: hidden; }`) and document separate layout profiles plus tall-page QA checks.
incidents.lessons_learned: Shared layout styles for immersive canvases can silently break analytics pages when global rules like `body { overflow: hidden; }` leak across views. Apply coordination + collaborative debugging (protocols 5 & 6), treat CSS/layout dependencies as first-class (protocol 14), and add regression guardrails (protocol 22) plus SL-1/SL-2/SL-3 shared-layout CSS protocols: separate layout profiles, tall-page QA checklist, and layout regression checks before reusing immersive styles.
